raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased bathroom tile from a company online. The tile was purchased through XXXX invoice. The tile we received was defective. I contacted the company to resolve it, but they were unwilling to resolve. I opened a claim with XXXX ; however, XXXX said they are not able to investigate and I need to file a chargeback with the credit card. On XX/XX/XXXX, I filed the chargeback with Citi, as this is the credit card used to pay the XXXX invoice. On XX/XX/XXXX, Citi informed me that they will close the case in the vendor 's favor, citing " With the information we had, it wasn't enough to support your claim and resolve the dispute in your favor ''. I had provided ample proof of the defective tile, as well as a direct communication with XXXX ( the merchant charging my card ) saying to file the chargeback. I believe Citi did not due any due diligence or investigation and closed the case. This is against their terms and conditions that protect consumers when using their credit cards.
